zh.Loudoun, Ayr, Scotland: 英國蘇格蘭艾斯爾盧度
zh.Lochawe, Argyll, Scotland: 英國蘇格蘭阿吉爾洛霍
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Andrew Campbell - Tenth Lord of Loudon於1310年出生在英國蘇格蘭艾斯爾時，他的父親Sir Duncan Campbell - Nineth Lord of Loudoun24歲，母親Susanna Crauford of Loudoun13歲。他和Campbell至少有4個兒子。他在蘇格蘭艾斯爾艾爾被列為sheriff。他去世於1368年3月4日之後在蘇格蘭艾斯爾盧度。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Andrew Campbell - Tenth Lord of Loudon于1310年出生在英国苏格兰艾斯爾时，他的父亲Sir Duncan Campbell - Nineth Lord of Loudoun24岁，母亲Susanna Crauford of Loudoun13岁。他和Campbell至少有4个儿子。他在苏格兰艾斯爾艾爾被列为sheriff。他去世于1368年3月4日之后在苏格兰艾斯爾盧度。

nameMeanings.name: Campbell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: nickname from Gaelic cam ‘crooked, bent’ + beul ‘mouth’. As a result of folk etymology, the surname was often represented in Latin documents as de bello campo ‘of the fair field’, which led to the name sometimes being ‘translated’ into Anglo-Norman French as Beauchamp .
Irish (North Armagh): adopted for Gaelic Mac Cathmhaoil ‘son of Cathmhaol’ (literally ‘battle chief’): see Caulfield and Cowell .
English: variant of   Camel  , under the influence of the Scottish name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Campell
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire) and English: variant of   Campbell   (see also   Camel  ).

variants.name: Beauchamp
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Antoine, Jacques, Yves, Andre, Armand, Emile, Fernand, Marcel, Marthe, Remi, Alcide, Camille.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of several places in France, for example in Manche and Somme, that are named with Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + champ(s) ‘field, plain’ (from Latin bello campo ‘beautiful field’). In English the surname is generally pronounced Beecham . Compare Bushaw .
English: in some cases, an Anglo-Norman French adaptation of Scottish Campbell , by translation into French of the folk etymology de campo bello ‘from the fair field’.

variants.name: Camel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Napoleon, Patrice.

English (of Norman origin) and French: nickname from Norman French came(i)l ‘camel’ (from Latin camelus, classical Greek kamēlos), possibly denoting a clumsy person or someone with long legs. It may also be a topographic or habitational name referring to a house with a sign depicting a camel (although surnames derived from house and inn signs are rare in English); compare 5 below.
English: possibly a habitational name from Queen Camel and West Camel in Somerset, early recorded as Camel(le), possibly a Celtic name from canto- ‘border, district’ and mēl ‘bare hill’.

nameMeanings.name: Andrew
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the Greek name Andreas, a short form of any of various compound names derived from andr- ‘man, warrior’. In the New Testament this is the name of the first disciple to be called by Jesus. After the Resurrection, St Andrew preached in Asia Minor and Greece. He is traditionally believed to have been crucified at Patras in Achaia. He was one of the most popular saints of the Middle Ages and was adopted as the patron of Scotland, Russia, and Greece. It has long been among the most popular boys' names in the English-speaking world, especially in Scotland. Its popularity in England was further enhanced by its use as a British royal name for Prince Andrew ( b. 1960 ), the Duke of York.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Andrew Campbell - Tenth Lord of Loudon was born in 1310, in Ayrshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, his father, Sir Duncan Campbell - Nineth Lord of Loudoun, was 24 and his mother, Susanna Crauford of Loudoun, was 13. He had at least 4 sons with Campbell. His occupation is listed as sheriff in Ayr, Ayrshire, Scotland. He died after 4 March 1368, in Loudoun, Ayrshire, Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Andrew Campbell - Tenth Lord of Loudon est né en 1310, à Ayrshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir Duncan Campbell - Nineth Lord of Loudoun, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Susanna Crauford of Loudoun, avait 13 ans. Il a eu au moins 4 fils avec Campbell. Sa profession de son indiquée est sheriff à Ayr, Ayrshire, Écosse. Il est décédé après 4 mars 1368, à Loudoun, Ayrshire, Écosse.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Andrew Campbell - Tenth Lord of Loudon 1310 in Ayrshire, Schottland, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Duncan Campbell - Nineth Lord of Loudoun 24 und seine Mutter Susanna Crauford of Loudoun 13. Er hatte mit Campbell mindestens 4 Söhne. sein Beruf ist als sheriff in Ayr, Ayrshire, Scotland aufgeführt. Er starb nach 4. März 1368 in Loudoun, Ayrshire, Scotland.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Andrew Campbell - Tenth Lord of Loudon è nato il nel 1310 nel Ayrshire, Scozia, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Sir Duncan Campbell - Nineth Lord of Loudoun, aveva 24 anni e il suo madre, Susanna Crauford of Loudoun, aveva 13 anni. Lui avuto almeno 4 figli con Campbell. La sua occupazione è indicata come sheriff nel Ayr, Ayrshire, Scotland. Morì dopo il 4 marzo 1368, nel Loudoun, Ayrshire, Scotland.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Andrew Campbell - Tenth Lord of Loudonが1310年にイギリススコットランドエイルシュアイアで生まれた時，父Sir Duncan Campbell - Nineth Lord of Loudounは24歳，母Susanna Crauford of Loudounは13歳であった。彼はCampbellとの間に少なくとも4人の息子をもうけた。スコットランドエイルシュアイアエイルでの職業はsheriffと記録されている。彼は1368年3月4日以後にスコットランドエイルシュアイアロウドウンで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Andrew Campbell - Tenth Lord of Loudon(이)가 1310년 영국 스코틀랜드 에이르실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Duncan Campbell - Nineth Lord of Loudoun(은)는 24세였고, 그의 어머니 Susanna Crauford of Loudoun(은)는 13세였다. 그는 Campbell(와)과 적어도 네 명의 아들을 두었다. 그의 직업은 스컷랜드 에이르실르 에이르에서 sheriff이었다. 그는 1368년 3월 4일 이후 스컷랜드 에이르실르 루다운에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Andrew Campbell - Tenth Lord of Loudon nasceu em 1310, em Ayrshire, Escócia, United Kingdom, seu pai, Sir Duncan Campbell - Nineth Lord of Loudoun, tinha 24 anos e sua mãe, Susanna Crauford of Loudoun, tinha 13 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 4 filhos com Campbell. Sua profissão aparece como sheriff em Ayr, Ayrshire, Scotland. Ele faleceu após 4 de março de 1368, em Loudoun, Ayrshire, Scotland.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Andrew Campbell - Tenth Lord of Loudon родился в 1310 г. в местности аиршир, Шотландия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Sir Duncan Campbell - Nineth Lord of Loudoun было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Susanna Crauford of Loudoun, 13. У него было по крайней мере 4 сына от Campbell.  Он работал по профессии sheriff в местности аир, аиршир, Шотландия. Он умер после 4 марта 1368 г. в местности лоудоун, аиршир, Шотландия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Andrew Campbell - Tenth Lord of Loudon nació en 1310, en Ayrshire, Escocia, Reino Unido, su papá, Sir Duncan Campbell - Nineth Lord of Loudoun, tenía 24 años y su mamá, Susanna Crauford of Loudoun, tenía 13 años. Tuvo por lo menos 4 hijos con Campbell. Su ocupación registrada era sheriff en Ayr, Ayrshire, Scotland. Murió después de 4 de marzo de 1368, en Loudoun, Ayrshire, Scotland.
